input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-02T02:36:37.116Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute being removed. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have and provide the replacement.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (which also gives you better control over which line should throw). Let me update your code:
data.reasoningText: MSTest v4 removed the `[ExpectedException]` attribute. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which existed since v2). Let me update the test file to use `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` which is the modern MSTest v4 approach, and also update the package version to v4.

toolCalls.name: edit
